name: Windshield GT (Ground Truth) — the corroborated per-session reconciler: Observe once → Decide purely → Apply serially, claiming only guarantees the store and runtime actually provide
northStar: Observe the world once and honestly per pass (doubt is Unknown, never Dead), decide purely over typed per-session state, and let every destructive or lossy action exist only as a value carrying proof of corroborated observation — claiming exactly the guarantees the real Dolt/sqlite store and tmux runtime provide, and not one more.
essentialJobShape: Make the live runtime match the ledger and config, one session at a time, without ever destroying on doubt. Per pass: decode each session bead once — totally — into typed desired facts (spec) and observed facts (status); observe the runtime through one frozen batch probe into honest tri-state liveness where fetch errors, stale caches, and single-list absence are Unknown, not Dead; compute, purely, the ordered idempotent actions repairing each durable-vs-observed disagreement (plus a later fleet stage for pool demand, computed over the projected post-plan world); execute serially, marker-last, with two-phase intent around lossy effects and proof objects around destructive ones. When ledger, runtime, and config agree, the action list is empty. Boot, crash recovery, and steady state are the same pass. Everything else in the 14,400 LOC is accidental complexity.

corePrimitives.name: FrozenObservation (corroborated tri-state probe)
corePrimitives.signature: func Observe(ctx context.Context, deps ObserveDeps) (Observation, error) — exactly one batch list-sessions + one proctable scan per pass, frozen; type Liveness int // Alive | Dead | Unknown; type RuntimeFacts struct{ Live Liveness; ConsecutiveMisses int; PID PIDFacts /*start-time+cmdline*/ }
corePrimitives.responsibility: The only impure read. Source-level tri-state: fetch error, staleTTL, or mid-pass cache refresh yields Unknown; a config-known key absent from a SUCCESSFUL frozen list is Unknown-until-corroborated. Dead requires N consecutive successful-list absences AND a per-key HasSession confirm AND a proctable cross-check at mint time.
corePrimitives.replaces: Liveness{Running,Alive} bool pair (internal/runtime/liveness.go:8-9), StateCache staleTTL/error→Running=false collapse, the separate workerSessionTargetRunning/workerObserveSessionTarget re-probes, and lazy 2s-TTL mid-pass refreshes that tear the fleet view.
corePrimitives.sourceThesis: Windshield's Snapshot, rebuilt per the distributed-systems red-team blocker 1: the witness types were blind to false-negative list absence; tri-state must be real at the source, not just in the Decide type.

corePrimitives.name: Typed session codec (total decode, real state space)
corePrimitives.signature: package internal/sessioncodec — func DecodeSpec(meta map[string]string) SessionSpec; func DecodeStatus(meta map[string]string) SessionStatus; type SessionState int enumerating ALL 13 observed states (active, creating, idle, pending, orphan, draining, ghost, quarantined, sleeping, dead, terminated, waiting, starting) + StateUnrecognized{Raw string; FirstSeen time.Time}; exhaustive-linted switches
corePrimitives.responsibility: Confine the raw metadata map to one package; everything above sees typed Spec/Status. Decode is total — unknown strings become a first-class typed state whose only legal outputs are defer + TTL + typed escalation event, never destructive action and never quarantine (rolling-deploy forward compatibility).
corePrimitives.replaces: ~29 raw metadata keys as decision inputs, the raw/Info predicate twins with 'equivalence-proven' comments, knownSessionStates allowlist, and (gradually, through phases) the 50-field Info mirror and its ~3,019 read sites.
corePrimitives.sourceThesis: Windshield's spec/status split; the sealed 6-method handler interface is DROPPED per the YAGNI red-team — a 13→6 partition relocates complexity into OnActive/OnUnrecognized. A Go enum over the real state space plus the exhaustive lint gives compile/CI-forced totality without inventing a taxonomy.

corePrimitives.name: Per-session pure core (grown, not replaced)
corePrimitives.signature: func deriveSessionActions(d durableFacts, r runtimeFacts, f FleetFacts) []sessConvergeAction — keeps the landed signature at cmd/gc/session_level_converge.go:214, widened with one read-only frozen fleet aggregate; determinism-linted (no time.Now, io, rand, goroutines)
corePrimitives.responsibility: The single auditable per-session decision surface. Every branch must cite the durable-vs-observed disagreement it repairs. Idle/sleep/wake arms stay bead-state-keyed (the #312 lesson) — no `if idle > N then act` heuristic may enter the core; time-threshold concerns escalate via event to prompts.
corePrimitives.replaces: The 2,188-line Phase-1 continue-ladder of reconcileSessionBeadsTracedWithNamedDemand, arm by arm behind the S19 parity pin — rollback, identity heal, priming activation (activates the dormant #3849 fix), drain, close.
corePrimitives.sourceThesis: YAGNI red-team blocker 2 accepted in full: 'widen into whole-fleet Decide' was a category error. This IS S19 continued; whole-fleet Decide(Snapshot) Plan, the sealed handler graph, and the 11-variant Effect sum are cut from the critical path.

corePrimitives.name: FleetFacts two-stage pure pipeline (explicit rewrite, phase-gated)
corePrimitives.signature: func projectWorld(cells []SessionCell, sessionActions map[SessionKey][]sessConvergeAction) ProjectedWorld; func deriveFleetActions(p ProjectedWorld, desired DesiredWorld) []fleetAction
corePrimitives.responsibility: Pool min-floor, undesired-pool sweep, and wake fairness as pure functions over the post-plan PROJECTED world — replacing the 32 mid-tick infoByID.ApplyPatch mutations whose this-tick visibility the min-floor scan currently requires (the MinFloorCountReflectsMidTickClose* tests become table rows).
corePrimitives.replaces: sweepUndesiredPoolSessionBeads, the min-floor scan over mid-tick mutations, the wake/sleep loop, and the adoption-barrier/proc-reap engines as separate mutators.
corePrimitives.sourceThesis: Code-reality red-team must-fix: cross-session arms are a rewrite, not a widen. Honestly labeled as such, sequenced late (W5), with its own semantic-equivalence parity strategy instead of borrowing the per-arm pin's credibility.

corePrimitives.name: Witness types: DeathCertificate + LiveTargetWitness
corePrimitives.signature: type DeathCertificate struct{ key SessionKey; pass uint64; _ noCopy } — unexported constructor, minted ONLY by Observe on corroborated-Dead; type LiveTargetWitness — minted only on Observed+IdentityMatch; single-use, valid one pass; every kill site rechecks PID start-time/cmdline before signaling
corePrimitives.responsibility: Destructive effects (orphan-close, teardown, pane kill) are struct-unconstructible without positively corroborated observation from THIS pass. An errored, stale, or single-list-absent probe mints nothing.
corePrimitives.replaces: The providerAlive err→false collapse (session_reconciler.go:1538-1540) that authorizes orphan-closes of live sessions, plus the deferSessionClosesOnBoot/storeQueryPartial boolean guards.
corePrimitives.sourceThesis: The unanimous survivor across all four red-teams — kept, but the minting condition is hardened per RT3: corroboration (not single-list absence) plus PID-reuse recheck at the kill site. Applied to ALL destructive paths including unrewritten legacy arms (W2), so it protects code not yet migrated.

corePrimitives.name: IntentMarker two-phase + named receiver-idempotency contracts
corePrimitives.signature: type IntentMarker struct{ AttemptedAt, ConfirmedAt time.Time; Attempts int }; func (m IntentMarker) Eligible(now time.Time, b Backoff) bool — already landed; plus a per-effect contract table: Start → deterministic agent.SessionNameFor + provider ErrSessionExists with probe-confirmed (not stale-cache) IsRunning; Prime → bounded attempts with duplicates explicitly accepted and documented; Close → naturally idempotent
corePrimitives.responsibility: Bound every lossy effect to at-least-once with bounded retries, and NAME the receiver-side dedup mechanism for each non-idempotent effect. The marker alone does not solve delivery — the claim is scoped honestly: bounded at-least-once, never exactly-once.
corePrimitives.replaces: Stamp-primed-before-nudge at-most-once prompt loss (the S19 trap, #3872/#3279 class) and ad-hoc double-spawn guards that lean on stale IsRunning=false.
corePrimitives.sourceThesis: Landed S19 stage 1 (actionAttemptPrime/attemptEligible, proven working per RT verification); honesty contract added per RT3 blocker 4.

corePrimitives.name: ApplyStatus: single-writer, torn-proof, drift-loud
corePrimitives.signature: func (c *Codec) ApplyStatus(ctx context.Context, id beads.BeadID, s SessionStatus) error — encodes the whole struct, diffs before writing, writes via ONE SetMetadataBatch whose FINAL key gc.status.gen is a hash of the encoded struct; decode treats gen-mismatch as torn → no-status/Unknown → re-heal; pre-write read-back compare against the pass-start snapshot emits a typed session.status.drift event and re-observes instead of overwriting foreign writes
corePrimitives.responsibility: Sole post-create writer of gc.status.* (the serial reconciler pass); no per-key status-write API exists. gc.spec.* is create-once: the API/CLI synchronous create (session_resolution.go:349) remains the one shared spec-stamping site, preserving the synchronous-create contract and the in-flight worker-boundary migration; post-create spec mutations route through the codec.
corePrimitives.replaces: Scattered per-arrival-path SetMetadata stamping for status keys, the ApplyPatch snapshot-fold clobber path, and the fictional whole-value-atomicity of per-key write loops.
corePrimitives.sourceThesis: Rev-CAS is DELETED per three concurring red-team blockers: internal/beads exposes no per-bead revision (only assignee-scoped ReleaseIfCurrent; PRAGMA data_version is global), so 'clobber structurally impossible' was vaporware. Downgraded honestly to serial single-writer + read-back drift events; torn-write gen-trailer per RT3 blocker 3; spec/status ownership split per RT1 blocker 2.

corePrimitives.name: Quarantine Guard (intensity-only, durable-with-reset)
corePrimitives.signature: func (g *Guard) Admit(k SessionKey, now time.Time) Decision // Proceed | Backoff{Until} | Quarantine{TTL, EscalationEvent} — counts ONLY crash/restart intensity; StoreErr, ProviderFlap, and Unknown observations map to backoff and can never mint a quarantine; verdict persists as gc.status.quarantined_until with TTL + reset-generation; counters stay in-memory; first-class operator-reset effect (gc session reset) mirrors the circuit breaker's reset knob
corePrimitives.responsibility: Stop the bleeding on poison keys mechanically; escalate treatment (the judgment) via typed event + bead to prompts/humans. It must never grow a branch that reasons about WHY a key failed.
corePrimitives.replaces: crashTracker, the backoff maps, the session circuit-breaker singleton, and the unknown-state skip-forever branch. idleTracker's time-threshold judgment is NOT folded in — idle arms stay bead-state-keyed.
corePrimitives.sourceThesis: Windshield's guard with every RT2 must-fix incorporated: intensity-only scope, transient-error exclusion (kills the durable-spurious-quarantine wedge), and the operator escape hatch preserved as a typed effect rather than raw hand-edits.

corePrimitives.name: Flight recorder (late, small, WAL kept alive until then)
corePrimitives.signature: func RecordPass(w io.Writer, obs Observation, actions ActionSet, report ApplyReport) error — size-capped JSONL of decision INPUTS and outputs; gc trace becomes a projection over it, with a mapping layer to the existing 185-code taxonomy for operator continuity
corePrimitives.responsibility: Production telemetry that doubles as a replay corpus: every incident becomes a permanent table-test fixture. Ships only in the FINAL phase — the trace WAL stays fully alive and correct through the oracle phases because the differential oracle and on-call tooling depend on it.
corePrimitives.replaces: The 5,124-LOC bespoke trace WAL (segments, CRC32, corrupt-quarantine, head recovery, pruning) — eventually, after shadow logs confirm no remaining readers.
corePrimitives.sourceThesis: Windshield's recorder, resequenced per the YAGNI red-team's dependency finding: you cannot delete in Phase 6 the subsystem your Phase 2-3 safety net runs on.

newErrorKillingPrimitives.name: Corroborated DeathCertificate / LiveTargetWitness
newErrorKillingPrimitives.errorClassKilled: Orphan-close/teardown of a LIVE session via BOTH swallowed probe errors (tmux blip → providerAlive=false) AND false-negative batch-list absence (mid-spawn or race-truncated list-sessions) — gc-hz0nu, gc-kkgak, #2073, #2987 — plus PID-reuse kills on late teardown.
newErrorKillingPrimitives.howUnrepresentable: Destructive effect values have required witness fields with unexported constructors invoked only by Observe on corroborated-Dead: N consecutive absences from SUCCESSFUL frozen lists, per-key HasSession confirm, and a proctable cross-check at mint time. Errors, stale caches, and first absences yield Unknown, which mints nothing — the destructive value cannot be built; any bypass is a compile error. Kill sites recheck PID start-time/cmdline, so a one-pass-stale certificate cannot kill a reused PID.

newErrorKillingPrimitives.name: Status-generation trailer over one batch write
newErrorKillingPrimitives.errorClassKilled: Torn half-written status trusted by the next decode — crash between state=active and instance_token in the per-key write loop; the 'whole-value atomicity' fiction across all three stores (BdStore documents SetMetadataBatch as not-truly-atomic externally).
newErrorKillingPrimitives.howUnrepresentable: ApplyStatus is the only status-write API and it emits exactly one SetMetadataBatch whose final key is a hash of the encoded struct; decode treats a gen-mismatch as no-status → Unknown → heal. There is no API through which individual status keys can be written, so a torn struct can be produced by a crash but never CONSUMED as truth.

newErrorKillingPrimitives.name: Total typed decode + exhaustive-lint state enum
newErrorKillingPrimitives.errorClassKilled: Unknown-state skip-forever with no TTL or escalation, re-logged every tick (#2085, #2389, #1496, #1497) — WITHOUT reintroducing the rolling-deploy hazard of quarantining a newer controller's legitimate new states.
newErrorKillingPrimitives.howUnrepresentable: Unknown strings decode to first-class StateUnrecognized{raw, firstSeen}; the exhaustive-linted switch cannot omit it; its only constructible outputs are defer + TTL + typed escalation event — destructive actions require witnesses it can never mint, and quarantine requires crash intensity it never accrues. There is no default-continue arm left to write.

newErrorKillingPrimitives.name: IntentMarker pair + named receiver dedup per lossy effect
newErrorKillingPrimitives.errorClassKilled: Silent at-most-once prompt loss (stamp-before-nudge) and unbounded at-least-once redelivery across crashes — the S19 priming trap generalized to start/close/rollback/drift-relaunch (#3872, #3279).
newErrorKillingPrimitives.howUnrepresentable: The applier's only lossy-effect entry points stamp attempted BEFORE the I/O and confirmed after — call sites cannot reorder the pair; Eligible() converts crash windows to bounded backoff retries. Each non-idempotent effect declares its receiver dedup in a contract table (deterministic SessionNameFor + ErrSessionExists for start; explicitly-accepted bounded duplicates for prime). The claim is scoped honestly: bounded at-least-once, never exactly-once.

newErrorKillingPrimitives.name: Create-once spec + enqueue-only post-create arrivals
newErrorKillingPrimitives.errorClassKilled: Edge-triggered per-arrival-path metadata stamping — spawn/adopt/resume/drift/rollback each shipping its own subset of identity/priming keys (#3872, #3849-class, #1893) — and the dual-representation hand-coherence drift (#1029, #1938, #3892).
newErrorKillingPrimitives.howUnrepresentable: Post-creation, no arrival path has a status-write API: events, pokes, and fsnotify can only enqueue a key; status keys exist solely as whole-struct ApplyStatus from the serial pass, so 'path X forgot key Y' has no code path to live in. Spec keys are stamped exactly once by the one shared synchronous create site, then immutable outside the codec.

newErrorKillingPrimitives.name: Read-back drift event (the honest Rev-CAS replacement)
newErrorKillingPrimitives.errorClassKilled: SILENT clobber of foreign writes to gc.status.* (bd CLI, API handlers, out-of-band gc subcommands, caching-store writeback) — the ga-ebb62d/#1029 class.
newErrorKillingPrimitives.howUnrepresentable: Explicitly NOT made unrepresentable — the store has no per-bead revision, so no such structural claim is made. Made impossible to fail silently: ApplyStatus reads back and compares against the pass-start snapshot before writing; a foreign diff emits a typed session.status.drift event and triggers re-observe instead of overwrite. Upgrades to true CAS only if the gated per-bead-revision spike lands in beads.

newErrorKillingPrimitives.name: Pure-core signature + determinism lint
newErrorKillingPrimitives.errorClassKilled: TOCTOU disagreement between per-question re-probes inside one pass, and hidden nondeterminism (time.Now, rand, map iteration) breaking replay and shadow-diff.
newErrorKillingPrimitives.howUnrepresentable: deriveSessionActions admits no store, provider, clock, or context — there is no API through which a decision can re-probe; a vet-style CI gate forbids the core package from importing io/net/math-rand or calling time.Now; inputs are sorted frozen values, never maps.

newErrorKillingPrimitives.name: Marker-last commit parameter (per-domain idiom, not shared kit)
newErrorKillingPrimitives.errorClassKilled: Dedup/progress markers written before (or swallowed after) their guarded writes, creating crash windows that drop or replay work — including the VERIFIED live drift inside the 'sound blueprint' itself: convergence's terminate path writes state, closes the bead, then best-effort `_ = SetMetadata(last_processed_wisp)` outside handler.commit.
newErrorKillingPrimitives.howUnrepresentable: commit takes the marker as a dedicated trailing parameter and writes it last internally with a checked error; the drifted call sites are routed through it in W0. Stays convergence-local until a second domain adopts it — per the two-implementations rule.

stateModel: Bead metadata splits into three prefixes with explicit, honestly-scoped ownership. gc.spec.* (desired: name, alias, rig, pool slot, config hash, prompt ref, deleted): create-once — stamped by the single shared synchronous create path (API/CLI via session_resolution.go:349 stays, preserving the sync-create contract and the worker-boundary migration); post-create mutations only through the codec. gc.status.* (observed: 13-state typed lifecycle, hashes, instance token, intent markers, quarantined_until, gen trailer): written only by the serial reconciler pass as whole-struct ApplyStatus — single-writer is per-host serial exclusivity plus read-back drift events, stated as enforced convention, not a storage guarantee, until a per-bead revision exists. gc.ext.*: operator annotations, never read by the core. Decode is total; the raw map is confined to the codec package. In-memory state is fail-safe timing scratch (intensity and absence counters — a restart only DELAYS verdicts). Quarantine verdicts are durable with TTL + reset-generation.
controlLoopShape: Level-triggered, crash-only, serial. One pass = Observe (single frozen batch probe + total decode; the only impure read) → per-session pure core over each cell, plus the fleet stage over the projected post-plan world once W5 lands → serial Apply (marker-last, two-phase around lossy effects, witnesses around destructive ones; the only impure write) → record. The 30s ticker is a resync floor enqueueing all keys; bead events, fsnotify, and pokes are enqueue-only hints — arrivals never write. Per-key jittered exponential backoff plus a global start token bucket bound respawn herds; the queue dedups at fleet size. Boot is the first tick — the 6-phase startup ladder and separate recovery path are deleted because recovery IS convergence. A pass panic abandons only that pass; the Guard counts intensity and quarantines poison keys with TTL, escalation event, and operator reset. Deliberately no worker pool — parallelism is a deferred scheduling decision, revisited on measured fleet size.
testabilityArchitecture: Shipping gates (all real, none aspirational): (1) Table tests over the pure per-session core — one row per historical bug, widening the landed S19 tables in place; every incident becomes a permanent row. (2) Laws as property tests, no simulator needed: totality fuzz (never panic, never silent-skip), fixpoint idempotence (apply then re-observe yields empty actions), no-destruction-on-Unknown (vacuous by witnesses, fuzzed as a tripwire), action-order absoluteness. (3) The determinism lint that makes replay structural. (4) The effect-boundary differential oracle with a hard completeness gate: all ~57 legacy effect sites are interposed to emit plan steps at the write boundary — CI asserts 1:1 site-to-step coverage BEFORE any soak counts, because the existing best-effort `if trace != nil` stream provably under-reports and would green-light false parity. (5) A tmux/subprocess conformance suite at the probe/witness boundary — the Layer-0 seam where the confirmed bugs actually live; the fake provider is pinned to it. Stretch, explicitly not merge gates: seeded simworld DST as a nightly soak; flight-recorder replay of production passes. Parity pin: per-arm shadow-diff under a declared semantic-equivalence relation (legacy nondeterminism makes byte-identity unreachable), owner-signed per flip.
whatGetsDeleted: Eventually: the 2,188-line/28-param god function and its 5-overload chain (session_reconciler.go, build_desired_state.go, session_lifecycle_parallel.go shrink to Observe/Apply glue); the raw/Info predicate twins and their equivalence-oracle suites; the 72-arm ApplyPatch and 52 (corrected count) *Patch constructors; knownSessionStates; sweep/adoption-barrier/proc-reap as separate mutators; the startup ladder; the tracker singletons; the 5,124-LOC trace WAL — final phase only, after its oracle duty ends and shadow logs confirm no readers. Explicitly NOT built: whole-fleet Decide(Snapshot), the sealed handler graph, the Effect/Outcome sums, the shared mechanics kit, Rev-CAS, merge-gating simworld. Honest accounting: ~15K deleted across the arc; ~3,019 raw-metadata read sites migrate gradually through the codec; ~5-6K added.

migrationPlan.phase: W0 — standalone bug-kills (ships alone, no new architecture)
migrationPlan.goal: Route convergence's terminate path through handler.commit (fixes the verified swallowed-marker drift); tri-state liveness at the source (error/staleTTL→Unknown); PID start-time/cmdline recheck at kill sites.
migrationPlan.safety: Three independent, individually revertable fixes; existing convergence and reconciler suites pin; each is fail-safe-direction (destroy less on doubt).

migrationPlan.phase: W1 — typed codec over existing keys (absorbs S19 stage-2 scope)
migrationPlan.goal: SessionSpec/SessionStatus total decode, 13-state enum + StateUnrecognized(defer+TTL+event), predicates rewritten over typed state; raw map confined to the codec package.
migrationPlan.safety: No key renames; the existing raw/Info equivalence oracles pin the codec byte-for-byte before any predicate flips.

migrationPlan.phase: W2 — corroborated observation + witnesses on ALL destructive paths
migrationPlan.goal: One frozen batch probe per pass, consecutive-absence corroboration, DeathCertificate/LiveTargetWitness required by every destructive path — unrewritten legacy arms included.
migrationPlan.safety: Witnesses protect legacy code immediately; the only behavior change is deferring destruction under doubt — the fail-safe direction, table-tested.

migrationPlan.phase: W3 — differential oracle with a completeness gate
migrationPlan.goal: Interpose the ~57 legacy effect sites to emit plan steps at the effect/write boundary; CI asserts 1:1 site-to-step coverage before any soak counts.
migrationPlan.safety: Legacy logic untouched; oracle validity is itself gated, closing the false-green trap of the best-effort `if trace != nil` stream.

migrationPlan.phase: W4 — grow the per-session core arm-by-arm (IS S19 stages 3-5, resequenced)
migrationPlan.goal: Widen deriveConvergeActions arms — rollback, identity heal, priming activation (turns on the dormant #3849 fix), drain, close — behind the parity pin; delete each legacy arm on flip.
migrationPlan.safety: Per-arm shadow-diff against the W3 oracle under a declared semantic-equivalence relation; owner signs each flip; legacy arm deleted only after.

migrationPlan.phase: W5 — fleet arms as an explicit rewrite
migrationPlan.goal: Two-stage pure pipeline (session actions → projected world → fleet actions) replacing min-floor/sweep/wake and the 32 mid-tick infoByID mutations.
migrationPlan.safety: Own parity strategy; MinFloorCountReflectsMidTickClose* tests become projection table rows; honestly labeled rewrite, never borrowing W4's per-arm pin.

migrationPlan.goal: ApplyStatus as one SetMetadataBatch + gen trailer; read-back drift events; operator-reset effect; front door warns (later rejects) raw gc.status.* writes.
migrationPlan.safety: Diff-before-write keeps Dolt churn identical; warn-first preserves the operator escape hatch; rejection flips only after drift telemetry is quiet.

migrationPlan.goal: Tick becomes enqueue-all + serial drain; per-key jittered backoff; global start token bucket. No worker pool.
migrationPlan.safety: Scheduling-only change; converged-world equality asserted under permuted schedules and injected crashes in the property suite.

migrationPlan.goal: Remove legacy arms, Info mirror, equivalence oracles; WAL→flight recorder; gc trace becomes a projection with a 185-code mapping layer kept one release.
migrationPlan.safety: Only flip-proven-unreachable code removed; production shadow confirms no remaining WAL readers before deletion.

migrationPlan.goal: Per-bead-revision spike in beads (upgrades drift events to CAS); shared mechanics-kit extraction; simworld DST nightly soak; per-key worker pool.
migrationPlan.safety: Each gated on a proven substrate or a second real consumer, per the project's two-implementations rule — never on the critical path.

invariantLedger.invariant: ZERO hardcoded roles — no role name in Go
invariantLedger.howHonored: Cells, actions, and witnesses are role-blind lifecycle mechanics; DesiredWorld is a pure config projection; no role string appears in any type, effect, or table row.

invariantLedger.invariant: Keep judgment out of Go — transport/convergence, not reasoning
invariantLedger.howHonored: Every core branch cites the durable-vs-observed disagreement it repairs; idle/sleep arms stay bead-state-keyed (#312 lesson); the Guard counts intensity only and escalates treatment via event+bead to prompts.

invariantLedger.invariant: Beads are the universal persistence substrate
invariantLedger.howHonored: Spec, status, intent markers, and quarantine verdicts live in bead metadata; nothing durable exists outside the ledger; the bead is the process heap.

invariantLedger.invariant: Events are the universal outbound-notification mechanism
invariantLedger.howHonored: The applier fires typed registered events (drift, quarantine, close, unrecognized-state); the flight recorder is telemetry and replay corpus, never a signaling channel.

invariantLedger.invariant: Config is the universal activation mechanism
invariantLedger.howHonored: Backoff, TTLs, corroboration N, and start budgets all come from city/pack TOML; no constant encodes policy.
Observe and Apply are the only impure sites; the determinism lint makes I/O in the core package uncompilable.

invariantLedger.invariant: No upward layer deps; CLI and API are projections
invariantLedger.howHonored: The codec and core move into internal/ (verified cycle-free: internal/ has zero cmd/gc imports); cmd/gc thins to wiring; gc trace becomes a projection.
Every action executes with zero [[agent]] entries configured; removing an agent removes cells from observation, never a mechanism.

invariantLedger.invariant: The system converges because work persists; redundancy is reliability
invariantLedger.howHonored: Level-triggered resync re-derives everything from durable state; the fixpoint law makes repeated independent observation idempotent; boot equals the first tick.

invariantLedger.invariant: No status/PID/lock files — query live state
invariantLedger.howHonored: Liveness comes only from live probes with proctable cross-checks; absence counters are in-memory scratch; the recorder is never consulted for process state.

invariantLedger.invariant: No premature abstraction / S35: share idioms, not domains
invariantLedger.howHonored: Generic Engine, shared mechanics kit, and Rev-CAS are cut; commit stays convergence-local until a second consumer exists; state machines stay per-domain.

invariantLedger.invariant: Typed wire, typed events, atomic writes, no panics, errors with context
invariantLedger.howHonored: Closed action enums with exhaustive lints; single SetMetadataBatch writes with gen trailers; the core returns values, never panics; reports wrap errors with key context.

invariantLedger.invariant: Worker-boundary migration (in flight) — one session-creation write site
invariantLedger.howHonored: Spec create-once keeps session_resolution.go's synchronous create as the shared creation site, sequenced with rather than colliding against the worker.Handle boundary work.

openQuestions: Fund the per-bead revision spike in beads? It is the only path from read-back drift EVENTS to true CAS, and touches BdStore, NativeDoltStore, CachingStore, MemStore, and the bd CLI schema — a cross-repo cost only you can weigh against the residual multi-writer clobber window.

Ratify the spec-ownership call: this design keeps API/CLI synchronous create as the one shared gc.spec.* writer (create-once) rather than forcing async reconciler-mediated creation. Confirm, and confirm its sequencing inside the in-flight worker-boundary migration.

Quarantine durability: durable quarantined_until with TTL + reset-generation (recommended here, with transient-error exclusion killing the spurious-wedge risk) reverses the documented in-memory OTP restart stance — needs your explicit reversal or veto.

Who owns the per-arm semantic-equivalence relation for the W4 parity pin? Byte-identity is unreachable under legacy nondeterminism; proposal: the arm's author defines the equivalence classes, a second maintainer signs them in the flip PR.

bd front-door contract: how long does warn-only on raw gc.status.* writes last before rejection, and does `gc session reset` fully replace the operator hand-edit workflows documented around the circuit breaker?

Corroboration latency: N=2 consecutive absences at a 30s resync floor roughly doubles time-to-teardown for genuinely dead sessions; is ~60s acceptable, or should Observe add an immediate per-key confirm probe on first absence to recover latency?

Multi-replica hosted topology: the .gc/controller.lock flock fences per HOST only. Before hosted multi-replica ships, do we need a ledger-level leader lease, or do you declare single-controller-per-city an operational invariant and enforce it in the crucible provisioner?
gc trace vocabulary: keep the 185-code taxonomy mapping layer permanently for on-call continuity (reconciler-debugging.md), or one release only and retrain?

CI budget and flake-quarantine policy for the tmux conformance suite (real tmux, subprocess tier — the one suite covering the seam where the confirmed bugs live) and for the optional nightly DST soak.

S-series governance: this document supersedes the CONTENT of S19 stages 2-7 while keeping S19's numbering, parity pin, and landed deriveConvergeActions as the vehicle (W4 = S19 stages 3-5 resequenced). Confirm so versioning of the pin stays coherent.
